title: Perideridia kelloggii
intro: Perideridia kelloggii, also known as Kellogg's yarrow or Kellogg's lomatium, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and congestion. The bioactive constituents of Perideridia kelloggii include sesquiterpenes and volatile oils, including isobutyl amine and camphor, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory ailments, and it is also incorporated into salves and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Perideridia kelloggii is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family. It is native to the western United States and is a perennial herb. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Perideridia, which is a small group of flowering plants. The genus Perideridia is part of the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. Perideridia kelloggii is further classified as a member of the subfamily Apioideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies within the Apiaceae.
uses: Perideridia kelloggii, also known as Kellogg's yampah, is a plant used by indigenous communities for various medicinal purposes. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. Traditionally, the roots of Perideridia kelloggii were used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses and potential benefits of Perideridia kelloggii.
